title: Stisseria ambigua
intro: Stisseria ambigua, also known as Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its claw-shaped root system. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, with health benefits including pain relief, improved digestion, and reduced inflammation. Bioactive constituents of Stisseria ambigua include harpagoside, a glycoside compound responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other iridoid and phenolic compounds. Stisseria ambigua is commonly prepared as a dried root extract or capsule, as well as in herbal teas and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
taxonomy: Stisseria ambigua is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, which has since been merged into the family Apocynaceae.
